name: Crags of Los Yébenes Trail (PR-TO 15)
name_original: Cerro de la Umbría — circular desde Los Yébenes
description: Explore the moderate 6.3-mile Cerro de la Umbría circular hiking route near Los Yébenes, offering scenic views and historical windmills.
region_intro: Hiking around Orgaz offers diverse landscapes, from the mountainous Sierra de Yébenes in the south, with elevations up to 1,121 meters, to the flatter terrain of the Iberian Meseta in the north. The region is characterized by varied Mediterranean ecosystems and features like the historic Bridge of Five Eyes over the Riánsares Stream. These geographical elements provide a range of hiking experiences, from challenging ascents to scenic walks through natural surroundings. Best hiking trails around Orgaz The most popular hiking route is Cliffs of Los Yébenes Trail, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views and passes through diverse Mediterranean landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Hitos de Orgaz Trail, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This trail winds through the natural environment surrounding Orgaz, providing varied scenery. Local hikers also love the Route of the Visigothic past – circular from Casalgordo (PR-TO 17), a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through historic areas and natural surroundings, often completed in about 4 hours 28 minutes. Hiking around Orgaz is defined by the Sierra de Yébenes, the Riánsares Stream, and diverse Mediterranean landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more strenuous routes. The routes in Orgaz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Orgaz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Orgaz?
A: Orgaz offers a diverse selection of over 20 hiking routes. These trails cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy walks to more challenging treks through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners in Orgaz?
A: Yes, Orgaz has several easy hiking options suitable for beginners. There are 4 trails rated as easy, providing accessible ways to explore the natural beauty of the area without significant difficulty.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in the Orgaz area?
A: For those seeking a more strenuous experience, Orgaz does offer challenging hikes. The region's southern part borders the Sierra de Yébenes, featuring elevations up to 1,121 meters, which provides a mountainous backdrop for more demanding routes. There is at least one trail specifically rated as difficult.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Orgaz?
A: Yes, you can find circular hiking routes in Orgaz. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018737425/route-of-the-visigothic-past-circular-from-casalgordo-pr-to-17" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Visigothic past – circular from Casalgordo (PR-TO 17)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 10.6-mile trail leading through historic areas and natural surroundings.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Orgaz?
A: Hiking in Orgaz offers diverse Mediterranean landscapes. You'll encounter varied ecosystems, from the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Yébenes in the south to the flatter areas of the Iberian Meseta in the north. Trails often feature panoramic views, especially routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018395892/cliffs-of-los-yebenes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Los Yébenes Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are hiking trails in Orgaz suitable for families?
A: Many trails in Orgaz are suitable for families, especially the easier and moderate routes. The diverse landscapes provide engaging scenery for all ages. When planning, consider the distance and elevation gain to ensure it matches your family's activity level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Orgaz?
A: Yes, many hiking trails in Orgaz are dog-friendly. For example, the Orgaz Multi Trail, located near Los Yébenes, is specifically noted as allowing dogs. It's always a good idea to check specific trail regulations, but generally, the natural environment is welcoming to pets.

Q: What interesting historical sites or natural features can I explore near the trails?
A: Orgaz is rich in historical and natural points of interest. You can explore the well-preserved 14th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Orgaz&lt;/a&gt;, which many routes lead to. Additionally, the historic 18th-century 'Bridge of Five Eyes' over the Riánsares Stream is a notable feature that can be incorporated into scenic walks. The nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Yébenes&lt;/a&gt; also offers points of interest.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Orgaz?
A: The best time to hike in Orgaz is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, so plan accordingly.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Orgaz?
A: The hiking trails in Orgaz are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.9 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic Mediterranean landscapes, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water elements like streams or bridges?
A: Yes, the region features the Riánsares Stream, which is crossed by the historic 18th-century 'Bridge of Five Eyes.' This notable feature can be part of scenic walks, offering a pleasant water element to your hiking experience.

Q: Are there less crowded hiking options in Orgaz?
A: While popular routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018395892/cliffs-of-los-yebenes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Los Yébenes Trail&lt;/a&gt; attract many, Orgaz has over 20 trails, providing ample opportunity to find quieter paths. Exploring some of the less-trafficked moderate or easy routes can offer a more secluded experience, especially during off-peak times.
tips:
The windmills of Los Yébenes, in the province of Toledo, are a historic and scenic site worth visiting for their cultural, architectural, and natural value.
The windmills date back to the 16th century, although the first documentary references date back to the 19th century.
They are horizontal-axis windmills with movable caps, typical of La Mancha.
It is believed that Miguel de Cervantes saw them as a child while passing through the area, drawing inspiration for his work Don Quixote de la Mancha. [turismo.lo...yebenes.es]
With its 963 m high, the hill offers us spectacular views of the two slopes of the Sierra de los Yébenes.
One of the viewpoints along the route, to enjoy the views of the surrounding landscape. From them we can see the Castle of Mora, the Castle of Almonacid, the Sierra de Nambroca, Pico de Noez.

faqs.question: What is the estimated hiking time for this circular route?
faqs.answer: The estimated hiking time for this moderate circular route is approximately 2 hours and 47 minutes, covering a distance of about 10.2 kilometers (6.3 miles).

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a diverse landscape characteristic of the Montes de Toledo, including mountainous and forested areas, as well as valleys. You may encounter escarpments and paths requiring firm footing, but it is generally accessible for a moderate hike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can discover several notable features. These include the historic Los Yébenes Windmills, the Cerro de la Umbría summit, and a Natural viewpoint offering panoramic views of the Algodor Valley. You might also spot the 16th-century Ermita de San Blas and remnants of an old open-pit iron mine.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail are not detailed, generally, trails in natural areas like the Montes de Toledo are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and potential livestock, and to carry waste bags to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Los Yébenes?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Los Yébenes. You should be able to find parking within the town itself, close to the starting point of the circular route. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked or can be found near local amenities.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot in this region, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter offers cooler temperatures but can sometimes bring rain or colder conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike or the Montes de Toledo area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Cerro de la Umbría circular route or to access the general Montes de Toledo area where it is located. The natural landscapes are freely accessible for hikers.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. Approximately 22% of the trail follows part of the Camino Manchego de Santiago, and about 9% coincides with the Risco de Los Yébenes trail.

faqs.question: What amenities are available in Los Yébenes town?
faqs.answer: Los Yébenes, the starting and ending point of the hike, offers various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and pubs for refreshments, as well as accommodation options if you plan to stay overnight. It also has bus connections to Toledo.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate hike like this?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, it's advisable to wear comfortable hiking shoes with good grip, bring sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing layers for the weather. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any particularly challenging sections on the trail?
faqs.answer: While classified as moderate, the trail does involve an elevation gain of about 167 meters. Some sections, particularly those with escarpments or uneven terrain, may require firm footing. Overall, it's manageable for hikers with a good basic fitness level.
